vitals.start_format: Rolling, police-escorted peloton (not a scored race); riders must maintain a minimum group pace of 18 mph
vitals.entry_fee: $250 registration fee + $10,000 fundraising commitment (Road Challenge tier)
vitals.feed_zones: 2 rest stops per the current official schedule (Mack Cycle, Sweet Bird North) — the organizer's own overview and national listing say 3; treat the exact count as an unresolved organizer inconsistency

climate.description: Mid-November in Miami sits at the tail of hurricane season transitioning into the dry season — generally warm and humid, with real afternoon-shower risk.
challenges: Heat and humidity typical of South Florida in November
Open causeway and bridge crossings expose riders to coastal wind

terrain.surface: Fully paved urban and coastal roads with several bridge/causeway crossings

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 63. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 61. EDITORIAL WAVE 8 (2026-07-24): prestige raised 3→4 — verified via the official 2026 'Meet the Pros' roster (bestbuddieschallenge.org/miami/meet-the-pros/) that this event draws a genuinely exceptional pro-cycling guest list — Chris Froome, Alberto Contador, and Sir Bradley Wiggins are advertised for 2026 (all three are Grand Tour general-classification champions), and Mark Cavendish (the Tour de France's all-time stage-win record holder, not a GC champion) and Geraint Thomas (2018 Tour de France winner) are documented as having actually ridden in 2024 — a real and dated signal, not marketing puffery. New sum of 14 dimensions = 41 + cultural_impact 3 = 44; 44/70*100 = 62.86, rounds to 63. Tier remains T2. No other dimensions changed — see catalog_flags for a taxonomy note that several dimensions (field_depth, prestige, community_culture) assume a competitive format this event does not have.

climb_profile.profile_type: Flat South Florida course; only minor rises on bridge/causeway crossings (Julia Tuttle, Rickenbacker) — no verified elevation total exists

course_description.character: Best Buddies Challenge: Miami is a fully supported charity fundraising ride, not a competitive gran fondo. The Saturday road Challenge offers 100-mile and 75-mile options, starting and finishing at the Four Seasons Hotel Miami (1435 Brickell Avenue). The course loops through Brickell/Downtown Miami, across the Julia Tuttle Causeway to South Beach and Miami Beach, north to Bal Harbour, Haulover Beach and Aventura, and separately across the Rickenbacker Causeway to Virginia Key and Key Biscayne, plus Coral Gables and Calle Ocho — five total waterway crossings. Riders move together in a single rolling, police-escorted peloton — the organizer describes it as a 'presidential-style' enclosure with road and intersection closures, support vehicles, medical support, and mechanics — rather than a full-day closure of every road on course; a stated minimum group pace of 18 mph is required to stay with the peloton. A separately priced Sunday gravel event (54/28/16-mile loops from DiMare Farms in the Redlands) also exists as an add-on but is a different ride, not a shorter option for the Saturday road Challenge. No official numerical elevation figure is published for the road course — treat it as essentially flat, with only bridge/causeway crossings providing meaningful rises.
course_description.signature_challenge: There is no terrain-based signature challenge — the test is holding the enclosed peloton's required 18 mph minimum pace across roughly 100 flat, humid South Florida miles, not any single climb or feature.
suffering_zones: Sustaining the peloton's required minimum pace across open causeway crossings (Julia Tuttle, Rickenbacker) where coastal wind is the main physical variable on an otherwise flat course
Heat and humidity typical of a November morning-into-afternoon ride in South Florida
A long, uniformly flat course with nothing to break rhythm — the physical test here is holding pace for hours, not managing terrain

logistics.lodging_strategy: Two hotel nights at the Four Seasons Hotel Miami are included in the Road Challenge fundraising tier; the event starts and finishes at the Four Seasons (1435 Brickell Avenue).

history.origin_story: The Miami edition of the Best Buddies Challenge debuted in 2012 — a dozen years after the flagship Hyannis Port event (2000). Best Buddies International runs the ride to fund its intellectual and developmental disability (IDD) programs; the Miami weekend is paired with the separately ticketed Best Buddies Miami Gala.
notable_moments: 2026 'Meet the Pros' roster advertises Chris Froome, Alberto Contador, Sir Bradley Wiggins, Cam Wurf, and Leanda Cave as participants (not yet confirmed as having ridden).
2024 edition drew Mark Cavendish and Geraint Thomas; past editions have included George Hincapie, Christian Vande Velde, Tom Pidcock, Magnus Sheffield, and Bobby Julich.

2021 Challenge alone reportedly raised $1.5 million; combined Challenge-and-Gala weekend totals have reached as high as a record $7.2 million (2024 Gala) and a projected $5 million (2025) — these figures typically combine the ride with the separately ticketed gala and should not be read as cycling-only totals.
history.reputation: A small (50-rider), high-profile charity ride that draws a genuinely exceptional pro-cycling guest roster — multiple Tour de France champions have appeared — in service of a real fundraising mission, not a competitive-cycling reputation.

biased_opinion.verdict: An Elite-Studded Charity Ride, Not a Competitive Fondo — Know the Real Price of Entry
biased_opinion.summary: Best Buddies Challenge: Miami is a small, tightly capped (50-rider) charity fundraising ride benefiting Best Buddies International's intellectual and developmental disability programs — not a competitive gran fondo in any real sense. Riders move together in a rolling, police-escorted peloton at a required minimum pace, not a scored race, along a flat Miami/Miami Beach/Key Biscayne loop starting and finishing at the Four Seasons Hotel Miami. What sets it apart is a genuinely exceptional pro-cycling guest list — Chris Froome, Alberto Contador, and Sir Bradley Wiggins are advertised on the 2026 roster (not yet confirmed as having ridden), while Mark Cavendish (the Tour's stage-win record holder) and Geraint Thomas (2018 Tour de France winner) are documented as having actually ridden in 2024. The real barrier to entry isn't fitness, it's fundraising: the Road Challenge tier requires a $250 registration fee plus a $10,000 fundraising commitment, with Best Buddies charging the difference to your card if you fall short two weeks out. Course elevation is unverifiable and should not be trusted at a specific figure — this is a flat South Florida ride with a few bridge and causeway rises, nothing more.
strengths: A genuinely exceptional pro-cycling guest roster for a charity ride — Grand Tour champions Froome, Contador, and Wiggins are advertised for 2026, and Cavendish (Tour stage-win record holder) and Thomas (2018 Tour winner) are documented as having actually ridden in 2024, not just lent their names

Real, verifiable fundraising track record for a legitimate cause — Best Buddies International's IDD programs — with individual-year Challenge totals independently reported (e.g., $1.5 million in 2021)
Genuinely scenic South Florida course — Julia Tuttle and Rickenbacker causeways, Key Biscayne, South Beach, Coral Gables — with full police-escorted road support and a Four Seasons Hotel start/finish
Comprehensive included package for fundraisers who hit the commitment: kit, finish amenities, two Four Seasons hotel nights, and gala admission for the rider plus one guest
weaknesses: Not a competitive race — no classifications, prizes, or individual racing format; the enclosed peloton requires a minimum 18 mph pace to stay with the group, but nothing is scored

The real cost of entry is the fundraising commitment, not the $250 registration fee — the Road Challenge tier requires $10,000 in fundraising, and Best Buddies will charge your card the shortfall two weeks before the event if you haven't hit that number
Capped at just 50 riders — this is a small, curated event, not a mass-participation fondo

No verifiable elevation figure exists for the course, and the organizer's own materials are inconsistent even on basics like the number of rest stops (2 per the current schedule, 3 per the overview/national listing)
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Sign up if the cause matters to you, you can meet a five-figure fundraising ask, and you want a genuinely elite pro-cycling atmosphere on a flat, well-supported South Florida course. Skip it if you're looking for a scored, competitive gran fondo — that's not what this is, regardless of the pro names on the start list.

final_verdict.one_liner: A 50-rider charity peloton with Grand Tour champions on the guest list — the price of admission is a $10,000 fundraising ask, not a finish time.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you can meet the fundraising commitment and want a well-supported, flat South Florida ride alongside a genuinely elite pro-cycling guest list. No if you're looking for a scored, competitive event — there isn't one here.
final_verdict.alternatives: For the flagship, longer-running Best Buddies Challenge with a similar charity format: Best Buddies Challenge: Hyannis Port (Boston to Cape Cod, since 2000). For a competitive Miami-area gran fondo instead of a charity ride: look for USAC-sanctioned South Florida road events.

eligibility.notes: CORRECTIONS (2026-07-24, editorial wave 8): date corrected from 'November TBD' to the confirmed Saturday, November 14, 2026 (7:00am start). Distance options corrected/added: 100mi/160.9km and 75mi/120.7km (the prior file had only the 100mi figure with no route_options). Elevation (152m/500ft) removed — unverifiable; no official GPX or numerical ascent figure exists, and the route is substantively flat with only bridge/causeway rises. Founded corrected from 2006 to 2012 — Best Buddies' own milestone history confirms the Miami edition debuted in 2012 (the flagship Hyannis Port event began in 2000; the two should not be conflated). Field_size added (limited to 50 riders). Entry_fee added: $250 registration + $10,000 fundraising commitment (Road Challenge tier); Best Buddies charges the shortfall to the registered card if the commitment isn't met two weeks before the event. Purged the entire prior youtube_data block — none of the five videos were about this event (one was a Miami criterium race, one a Bowral Classic Australia gran fondo vlog, one a Kansas City gran fondo promo, and two were completely unrelated human-interest/bodycam news clips); the associated rider_intel/search_text had fabricated 'Best Buddies Challenge Miami' course details (a fictitious '150km' course with climbs) that were actually describing the unrelated Bowral Classic video — a clear contamination case, removed entirely rather than partially retained. DEDUPE CONFIRMATION: verified as a genuinely distinct event from 'Best Buddies Challenge: Hyannis Port' (different city, different date, different founding year — 2012 vs. 2000) per Best Buddies' own official series history; not a duplicate, no catalog_flags duplicate_of needed. See catalog_flags for a taxonomy note: this is a charity fundraising ride, not a competitive gran fondo.

catalog_flags.taxonomy_note: This is a charity fundraising ride, not a competitive gran fondo — participants move in a single rolling, police-escorted peloton at a required minimum pace (18 mph) with no classifications, prizes, or individual race format. The real barrier to entry is a fundraising commitment ($10,000 for the Road Challenge tier), not fitness or race-day performance. Confirmed as a genuinely distinct event from 'Best Buddies Challenge: Hyannis Port' (Boston-to-Cape-Cod, founded 2000) — Miami debuted in 2012 as a separate series entry, per Best Buddies' own milestone history; this is not a duplicate.
